label: The Escape Artist
gift: Honoring the wisdom of fear — finding the exit, the trapdoor, the well-timed retreat.

cards.name: Notice what your fear is protecting — Sense
cards.essence: In yourself: the wisdom of fear, sensing what is here.
steps: Notice what is actually here, sensing what is here.
Practice it within: what your fear is protecting.
Shadow check: the Perpetual Vanisher — leaving before it’s time.

cards.essence: For others: the wisdom of fear, sensing what is here.
steps: Notice what is actually here, sensing what is here.
Offer it to others: the trap closing on someone.
Shadow check: the Perpetual Vanisher — leaving before it’s time.

cards.name: Spot Your Dodge
cards.essence: Notice the plan you're avoiding because building it makes the need real.
steps: Scan for the task you keep sliding past (the budget, the page, the list).
Feel the avoidance in your body.
Name the dodge out loud and take the smallest next step.
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I not letting myself see about the fund because seeing it commits me?
cards.campaignQuestion: What structural step for the car am I quietly dodging?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing the avoided move before it silently sinks the campaign.
cards.remediation: Open the avoided thing for 5 minutes only.

tell.working: You name the dodge and touch it.
tell.performed: You make a new to-do list to avoid the old one.

contraindications: When you genuinely need rest, not another push
cards.example: You keep 'forgetting' to total the costs; you open the doc for five minutes.

cards.name: Receive Without Bracing
cards.essence: Let help land for the car without bracing for the catch.
steps: Notice the brace in your body when someone offers.
Breathe; let the offer in one inch further than feels safe.
Say a clean 'thank you' without deflecting.
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I protecting by refusing help — and is it still worth the cost?
cards.campaignQuestion: Whose offered help for the car am I deflecting out of habit?
cards.optimizesFor: Actually receiving the resources people offer, so the fund grows.
forbiddenMoves: Over-explaining why you don't really need it
failureModes: Turning help into a debt you must repay
cards.remediation: If you deflect, circle back: 'Actually — yes, that would help.'

tell.performed: You accept but immediately plan to pay it back double.

contraindications: When the 'help' has real strings you're right to refuse
cards.example: A friend offers $300; instead of 'oh you don't have to,' say 'thank you — that genuinely helps.'

cards.name: Wise Retreat or Just Scared?
cards.essence: Sort a genuine reason not to ask from plain exposure-fear.
steps: Feel the flinch and locate it in the body.
Ask the Escape Artist's question: what is this fear protecting?
Sort: a real reason (name it) vs. just being seen needing help (proceed anyway).
cards.primaryQuestion: Is this 'no' wisdom, or is it just fear wearing wisdom's coat?
cards.campaignQuestion: Where am I calling avoidance 'discernment' in the campaign?
cards.optimizesFor: Not abandoning a good ask because needing help feels exposing.
forbiddenMoves: Ghosting the campaign to dodge the feeling
Carrying others' guilt about your need
cards.remediation: Set a 10-minute timer; send one ask before it rings.

tell.working: You name the fear and move.
tell.performed: You generate three more reasons to wait.

contraindications: When the retreat really is the wise move — then rest without guilt
cards.example: You feel dread posting the fundraiser; you sort it: not danger, just exposure — you post.

cards.name: Stay for the Ask
cards.essence: Don't vanish in the sixty seconds before you send the ask.
steps: Feel the urge to flee right before sending.
Set a 10-minute timer; name the fear out loud.
Send one ask before it rings.
cards.primaryQuestion: Is my urge to bolt protecting me from real danger, or just from being seen needing help?
cards.campaignQuestion: Which ask am I one click away from sending but keep escaping?
cards.optimizesFor: Sending the ask — the move that could actually increase life — instead of fleeing it.
forbiddenMoves: Suddenly reorganizing your whole plan to avoid sending
cards.remediation: Shrink it: send the ask to one safe person first.

tell.working: You name the fear and send anyway.
tell.performed: You produce three more reasons to wait.

contraindications: When pausing is genuinely wiser — but be honest
cards.example: Timer set: text one person the fundraiser link before 10 minutes pass.

cards.name: Open a door for someone else — Edge

steps: Take the concrete act, at the edge, naming what resists.
Offer it to others: a door for someone else.
Shadow check: the Perpetual Vanisher — leaving before it’s time.

cards.name: Pre-Commit the Rest
cards.essence: Decide in advance when you'll rest, so you don't bolt from the campaign mid-push.
steps: Name the point where you'll pause (after the asks go out, not before).
Schedule the rest like an appointment.
When the urge to vanish hits early, feel it, point to the scheduled rest, and keep going.
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I about to call quitting 'self-care' to escape the exposure?
cards.campaignQuestion: What sustainable rhythm lets me keep asking without burning out or bolting?
cards.optimizesFor: Staying in the campaign by resourcing rest, so the effort can increase life rather than drain you.
forbiddenMoves: Pushing with no rest until you crash
cards.remediation: If you bolted, name the real fear and set the next small ask.

tell.working: You rest on schedule and return.
tell.performed: You 'rest' the moment it gets exposing.

contraindications: When you truly need to stop — then stop, cleanly
cards.example: Plan: send five asks, then take the weekend off, then resume Monday.

cards.name: Open a door for someone else — Structure
cards.essence: For others: the wisdom of fear, building it into structure.
steps: Take the concrete act, building it into structure.
Offer it to others: a door for someone else.
Shadow check: the Perpetual Vanisher — leaving before it’s time.

cards.name: Name the Cost of Staying
cards.essence: Name what staying in the ask actually costs you, so the cost is chosen, not leaked.
steps: Feel where the campaign drains you.
Name the real cost out loud (time, pride, exposure).
Decide if it's worth it — and if yes, stay on purpose.
cards.primaryQuestion: What is staying in this fundraiser costing me that I haven't named?
cards.campaignQuestion: What do I need to acknowledge about the cost so I can keep showing up honestly?
cards.optimizesFor: Chosen, sustainable presence instead of silent depletion.
forbiddenMoves: Turning your cost into others' obligation
cards.remediation: Name the cost to one trusted person; let it be witnessed.

tell.working: You stay clear-eyed about the price and stay anyway.
tell.performed: You keep score of what staying costs.

contraindications: When the cost is too high — then it's okay to step back
cards.example: Admit asking for money bruises your pride; name it, and choose to keep asking.
